payload.title: Analise critica do ESAA atual
payload.description: Executar uma analise critica completa do estado atual do ESAA neste repositorio. Ler os contratos canonicos em .roadmap, schemas, politicas, perfis, read models, roadmaps plugin, readme/AGENTS/CLAUDE quando existirem, implementacao do harness/runtime em src/esaa e testes em tests. Procurar falhas, bugs, inconsistencias entre configuracao, schemas, comandos CLI, harness/orchestrator, documentacao e comportamento observado. Validar comandos essenciais com PYTHONPATH=src quando seguro. Ao final produzir um relatorio completo em docs/qa/ESAA-current-critical-analysis.md contendo escopo, metodologia, evidencias, achados priorizados, riscos, inconsistencias, bugs potenciais, pontos fortes, insights arquiteturais, recomendacoes e proximas tarefas sugeridas. Nao editar .roadmap manualmente; qualquer conclusao deve ser evidence-based.

payload.notes: Analise critica completa do ESAA atual com achados priorizados e evidencias.

checks: behavioral reproductions for atomicity, review lock and plugin dispatch completed

payload.title: Governed admin bootstrap for contract edits
payload.description: Specify the one-time governance bootstrap required before tasks in this roadmap may change canonical contract files under .roadmap. The spec must define an explicit admin/orchestrator path that allows only .roadmap/AGENT_CONTRACT.yaml, .roadmap/ORCHESTRATOR_CONTRACT.yaml, .roadmap/agent_result.schema.json, .roadmap/roadmap.schema.json and required PARCER profiles. It must explicitly forbid writes to .roadmap/activity.jsonl, .roadmap/roadmap.json, .roadmap/issues.json, .roadmap/lessons.json, backups, snapshots and arbitrary .roadmap paths. Include acceptance criteria proving that the bootstrap is narrow, temporary, auditable and does not bypass append-only event-store governance.

payload.title: Verify governed admin bootstrap boundaries
payload.description: Verify that BOOT-1804 defines a safe bootstrap path for contract edits without permitting direct event-store or read-model mutation. The QA report must enumerate allowed and forbidden paths, confirm that activity.jsonl and projections remain single-writer/derived artifacts, and state whether the later .roadmap contract edits in this roadmap are executable only through the declared bootstrap. No source code implementation is required in this task.

payload.title: Serializable append lock policy
payload.description: Specify the serializable append model for the event store. The policy must move from byte-level append locking to a transaction that covers parse, project, validate expected event sequence, assign final event_seq/event_id values, append events and save projections. Define expected inputs such as expected_first_seq and expected_projection_hash, stale-state rejection behavior, retry guidance, and invariants: no duplicate event_seq, no gaps, no stale projection write, and no direct mutation outside the Orchestrator path.

payload.title: Implement serializable append transaction
payload.description: Implement the serializable append policy from FIX-1806. Add a transactional store API that acquires the activity lock before deciding final event_seq values, rereads activity.jsonl inside the lock, validates the expected sequence and projection hash, assigns contiguous event ids, appends events, materializes read models and writes projections under the same critical section. Refactor service paths that currently compute event_seq before append so they use this API. Preserve existing command outputs and structured ESAAError codes for stale state and lock timeout.

payload.title: Verify serializable append transaction
payload.description: Create tests and QA evidence for the serializable append transaction. Cover two concurrent processes attempting to append from the same starting state, stale expected sequence rejection, projection hash revalidation, lock timeout behavior, no duplicate event_seq, no gaps and verify_status=ok after successful concurrent operations. The QA report must include command output summaries and explain why byte-level append locking alone was insufficient.

payload.title: Atomic complete and file effects policy
payload.description: Specify atomic handling of complete plus file effects. The policy must guarantee that final files are never written unless the corresponding complete and orchestrator.file.write events have been admitted. Define staging, validation, transactional append, final atomic rename/application, recovery behavior after interruption, cleanup of abandoned staged artifacts, and acceptance criteria for append failure, lock timeout and partial-write prevention.

payload.title: Implement atomic complete and file effects
payload.description: Implement the atomic complete/file effect policy from FIX-1805 using the serializable transaction API from FIX-1806-IMPL. Stage file_update contents outside final paths, validate boundaries and write conflicts before admission, append complete and orchestrator.file.write events transactionally, then apply final filesystem changes only after the event-store append succeeds. Ensure append failure, stale sequence and lock timeout leave no final output file behind.

payload.title: Verify atomic complete and file effects
payload.description: Create tests and QA evidence proving that file side effects cannot appear without admitted ESAA events. Cover lock timeout before append, stale expected sequence, simulated append failure, successful complete with file write, staged artifact cleanup and verify_status=ok. The key regression assertion is that a final docs/src/tests output path does not exist when complete returns STORE_LOCK_TIMEOUT or stale-state error.

payload.title: File effect artifact and hash audit policy
payload.description: Specify forensic audit metadata for file effects. The policy must extend orchestrator.file.write to record path, before_sha256, after_sha256, bytes, encoding, artifact_sha256 and artifact_path. Define the content-addressed artifact format under .roadmap/artifacts/file-effects/<sha256>.json, including content or patch payload, canonical hash inputs, missing-artifact behavior, replay verification and retention rules. This task depends on atomic file effects because artifacts must describe admitted effects only.

payload.title: Implement file effect artifacts and hashes
payload.description: Implement the file effect artifact policy from FIX-1808. Generate content-addressed artifact records before final application, compute before/after hashes and byte counts, include artifact metadata in orchestrator.file.write events, and expose helper validation for replay/audit. Ensure artifact writes are coordinated with the atomic complete transaction so an artifact cannot claim an effect that was not admitted.

payload.title: Verify file effect artifact auditability
payload.description: Create tests and QA evidence for file effect artifact auditability. Cover successful artifact creation, before/after hash correctness, bytes and encoding fields, missing artifact detection, hash mismatch detection, replay/audit helper output, and verify_status=ok after normal writes. The QA report must show an example orchestrator.file.write payload with the new metadata.

payload.title: Independent QA review role policy
payload.description: Specify the review role policy that aligns runtime behavior with the ESAA contract. complete must remain restricted to the actor that claimed the task, but review must be authorized by QA role or policy rather than by the completion owner. Define actor-role resolution, default QA actors, expected rejection code for non-QA reviewers, compatibility impact on existing tests and docs, and the exact contract/doc updates needed after the BOOT-1804 bootstrap.

payload.title: Implement independent QA review policy
payload.description: Implement independent QA review. Refactor projector/service validation so review approval no longer requires assigned_to ownership, while complete still enforces assigned_to == actor. Add role resolution for agent-qa through agents_swarm or runtime policy. Update CLI examples and contract files allowed by the bootstrap so docs and runtime agree. Existing owner-review tests must be changed to assert rejection unless the owner also has QA role.

payload.title: Verify independent QA review policy
payload.description: Create tests and QA evidence for independent QA review. Cover agent-spec claim/complete followed by agent-qa review approve, owner review rejected when owner lacks QA role, request_changes by agent-qa returns to in_progress, complete by non-owner still rejected, and documentation examples matching the runtime. The QA report must include before/after behavior for the previous LOCK_VIOLATION conflict.

payload.title: Unified eligible and run plugin dispatch policy
payload.description: Specify one canonical eligibility view shared by eligible and run. The spec must require run to consume tasks_with_planned_plugins or equivalent, admit plugin tasks through deterministic task.create before claim, preserve event-store authority after admission, respect dependencies and parallel_groups, and report source=roadmap_plugin before admission and source=event_store after admission.

payload.title: Implement unified eligible and run plugin dispatch
payload.description: Refactor run so it uses the same planned-plugin task view as eligible. Before claiming a plugin-only task, append a deterministic task.create admission event using the same validation path as claim_task. Ensure run --parallel still groups by planned write sets, handles dependencies, and does not silently prefer seed/event-store tasks over lexicographically earlier plugin tasks.

payload.title: Verify run consumes roadmap plugin tasks
payload.description: Create tests and QA evidence proving eligible and run use the same plugin task universe. Cover a plugin task listed by eligible and then claimed by run, deterministic task.create admission before claim, dependency blocking across plugin and event-store tasks, parallel wave grouping, and verify_status=ok after run.

payload.title: Done status evidence for issue.report
payload.description: Specify how issue.report preserves the real status when invoked on a done task. The preferred design is to allow prior_status=done only for issue.report; if schema compatibility requires otherwise, define an observed_task_status field with const done. The spec must remove the current done-to-review masking behavior, update schema/contract wording, and define validation rules so claim/complete/review over done remain forbidden.

payload.title: Implement done status evidence for issue.report
payload.description: Implement the FIX-1810 schema and runtime behavior. Update agent_result.schema.json and validation so issue.report can preserve done status evidence, remove service.report_issue masking from done to review, keep claim/complete/review over done rejected, and update docs/contracts under the BOOT-1804 allowlist. Ensure existing issue.report flows in todo, in_progress and review remain valid.

payload.title: Verify issue.report preserves done status
payload.description: Create tests and QA evidence for issue.report on done tasks. Cover done task issue.report payload preserving done status, claim/complete/review rejected on done, schema rejection for invalid prior_status/action combinations, existing non-done issue.report compatibility, and verify_status=ok after the issue event.

payload.title: Hotfix create validation policy
payload.description: Specify validation for hotfix.create. The policy must require an existing open issue, a fixes target that references an existing task, and for immutable-done hotfixes the original task must be done. Define structured error codes for issue missing, issue not open, fixes task missing, fixes task not done, duplicate hotfix and invalid scope_patch. Preserve the existing valid flow issue.report -> hotfix.create -> claim -> complete -> review -> issue.resolve.

payload.title: Implement hotfix create validation
payload.description: Implement the hotfix.create validation policy from FIX-1811 in service/build_hotfix_event paths. Validate issue existence and open status from projected issues, validate fixes task existence and done status when required, reject duplicate hotfixes as before, validate scope_patch is non-empty and normalized, and return short structured ESAAError codes suitable for CLI output.

payload.title: Verify hotfix create validation
payload.description: Create tests and QA evidence for hotfix.create validation. Cover issue nonexistent, issue already resolved, fixes task nonexistent, fixes task not done, empty/invalid scope_patch, duplicate hotfix and a valid hotfix for an open issue on a done task. The QA report must include the exact error codes and show the valid flow still reaches verify_status=ok.

payload.title: runner.metrics reserved action contract
payload.description: Specify runner.metrics as a reserved Orchestrator action. The spec must define its authority boundary, minimum payload fields or reference to runner_metrics.py, relationship to external runners such as Claude Code, Codex and Antigravity, and the documents/contracts that must list it: AGENT_CONTRACT, ORCHESTRATOR_CONTRACT, AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, readme and constants. The action must remain forbidden for agent outputs.

payload.title: Align contracts with runner.metrics
payload.description: Update canonical contracts and operational documents so runner.metrics is listed as a reserved Orchestrator action everywhere the reserved action vocabulary appears. Keep it out of allowed_agent_actions and agent_result.schema.json action enum. Update the contract consistency checker so missing runner.metrics is reported as drift.

payload.title: Verify runner.metrics contract alignment
payload.description: Create tests and QA evidence that runner.metrics is consistently reserved to the Orchestrator. Cover contract checker failure when runner.metrics is removed from one canonical source, success when all sources align, agent output rejection if runner.metrics is attempted as an agent action, and existing runner metrics CLI behavior remaining valid.

payload.title: Baseline lessons reseed policy
payload.description: Specify how baseline lessons LES-0001, LES-0002 and LES-0003 are reconstructed after init and activity clear. The preferred design is to reseed them with an event that materialize can replay, using orchestrator.view.mutate with a lessons payload if no new action is needed. Define canonical lesson ids, titles, rules, scopes, enforcement modes, source refs and acceptance criteria for dispatch-context injection after reset.

payload.title: Implement baseline lessons reseed
payload.description: Implement baseline lessons reseed through replayable events. Add a canonical baseline lesson builder, make init and activity clear append or otherwise materialize the baseline lessons through the approved event path, and ensure dispatch-context includes active LES-0001, LES-0002 and LES-0003 after reset. Do not hand-edit lessons.json. Update documents that previously described active lessons as static if needed.

payload.title: Verify baseline lessons reseed
payload.description: Create tests and QA evidence for baseline lessons reseed. Cover init, activity clear, project/replay, lessons.json content, dispatch-context lesson injection for applicable task kinds, no manual read-model edits, and verify_status=ok. Confirm LES-0001, LES-0002 and LES-0003 survive deterministic projection.

payload.title: Critical findings audit coverage specification
payload.description: Specify expanded audit coverage for all critical findings from docs/qa/ESAA-current-critical-analysis.md. The criteria must cover atomicity of file effects, serializable event-store locking, independent QA review policy, run/eligible plugin parity, issue.report on done preserving status, hotfix.create validation, runner.metrics contract alignment and baseline lessons reseed. Define expected finding ids, severity levels and evidence fields for each checker.

payload.title: Implement critical findings audit coverage
payload.description: Expand src/audit checkers or add a new checker so the audit suite detects every finding specified by AUD-1814. The implementation must inspect contracts, schemas, runtime code patterns and tests where appropriate. It must detect missing runner.metrics in reserved actions, review role drift, absence of baseline lesson reseed, hotfix validation gaps, plugin dispatch parity gaps, file effect audit metadata gaps and non-serializable append patterns.

payload.title: Verify critical findings audit coverage
payload.description: Create final QA tests and report for the critical findings audit suite. Run the full pytest suite, all audit scripts, roadmap schema validation and esaa verify. Add regression fixtures proving each audit finding is detected when a source is intentionally missing or inconsistent. The QA report must summarize final command outputs and confirm that the new roadmap's original findings are now covered by automated audit checks.

payload.title: External runner telemetry contract
payload.description: Especificar a telemetria real de execucao para runners externos (Claude Code, Codex, Antigravity ou outro executor que abre o projeto e usa a CLI do esaa-core). A especificacao deve deixar claro que adapters nativos Anthropic/OpenAI/Gemini sao opcionais; o requisito obrigatorio e registrar metricas do ciclo executado pelo runner externo sem quebrar o modelo single-writer. Definir campos minimos: task_id, actor, runner_id, runner_kind, model, command_surface, started_at, ended_at, latency_ms, input_tokens, output_tokens, total_tokens, cost_estimate, status, error_code opcional e correlation_id. Definir como essas metricas entram no event store por acao reservada do Orchestrator ou por comando deterministico validado, como aparecem em esaa metrics, como lidar com valores desconhecidos (null, nunca inventar), e quais invariantes devem ser preservadas: append-only, event_seq monotonic, nenhuma edicao manual de read models e nenhuma dependencia de provider API nativo.

payload.title: Production hotfix trace scenario
payload.description: Especificar um cenario demonstravel de hotfix que apareca como trilha real de eventos ESAA, nao apenas como teste unitario. O fluxo minimo deve ser: issue.report -> hotfix.create -> claim -> complete com pelo menos 2 verification.checks -> review approve -> issue.resolve -> verify.ok. Definir se o cenario roda em fixture temporaria, workspace real ou ambos; quando rodar no workspace real, deve usar apenas comandos oficiais do esaa-core e nunca edicao manual de activity.jsonl. Definir dados canonicos do issue, nome do hotfix task, scope_patch permitido, arquivo de patch seguro em src/hotfix/ ou fixture equivalente, criterios de aceite e evidencias esperadas em metrics/eligible/verify. Explicitar que a tarefa done original permanece imutavel e que o issue.resolve e indispensavel para fechar a projecao de issues.

payload.title: Concurrent write conflict policy
payload.description: Especificar a politica completa de conflito para execucao concorrente com parallel_groups. A especificacao deve definir write_set planejado (outputs.files da tarefa), write_set efetivo (file_updates.path do complete), conflito por igualdade de arquivo e por prefixo quando um output representa diretorio, e comportamento esperado antes e depois do dispatch. Definir regras: tarefas com write_set planejado sobreposto nao entram no mesmo parallel_group; completes concorrentes sao serializados no append; se o write_set efetivo conflitar com evento ja admitido na mesma wave, o Orchestrator deve rejeitar ou reserializar conforme politica declarada; nenhum arquivo pode ser escrito antes de validacao de boundary e conflito. Incluir exemplos com duas tarefas escrevendo o mesmo arquivo, diretorios distintos, diretorio pai/filho e hotfix scope_patch.
